findings.evidence: Lines 17-18 define the positive part as coefficients with `0 < coeff D x`, and lines 40-42 define `negativePart` as the positive part of `-D`, so the docstring’s zero/pole labels are swapped.
findings.fix: Change this phrase to “positive/zero and negative/pole decomposition” or avoid the zero/pole terminology in this formal combinatorial file.
findings.issue: The module docstring says the file supplies a “positive/pole and negative/zero decomposition”, but for Weil/principal divisors positive coefficients correspond to zeros and negative coefficients to poles.

summary: The new file has a module docstring and docstrings for the substantive declarations, but the module docstring reverses the geometric terminology for zeros and poles.
